subject: [PATCH v4 0/4] sunrpc: hardcode pool_mode to pernode, remove other modes

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary:
Reviewer NeilBrown questioned the choice of commit to hardcode pool_mode to pernode, suggesting that it may not have ever worked correctly and recommending a different commit (3262c816a3d7) as the fix for the issue.

Reviewer suggested using a WARN_ON_ONCE() to defensively program against potential issues, considering it a sensible approach.
reviews.raw_body:
Why that commit?  Did this ever work correctly?
It seems more likely that 
Fixes: 3262c816a3d7 ("[PATCH] knfsd: split svc_serv into pools")
is appropriate.

---

Would a WARN_ON_ONCE() be appropriate here?

I think this is a sensible defensive-programming approach.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thnaks,
NeilBrown

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown suggested simplifying the code by removing the sv_nrpools field and only using the sv_is_pooled flag, as it is always true when either sv_nrpools is 1 or equal to svc_pool_map.count.
reviews.raw_body:
I think this patch is good, but there is probably room for further
simplification which is probably best left to a later patch.
In particular, ->sv_nrpools is now either '1' or svc_pool_map.count,
and these possibilities match ->sv_is_pooled.  So we only need one of
those.
I think removing ->sv_nrpools and only using ->sv_is_pooled would
simplify/clarify some of the code.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks,
NeilBrown

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown noted that the condition in svc_pool_map_init_pernode() is unnecessary because for_each_node_with_cpus() ensures each node allocated a pool has at least one CPU, and suggested addressing the problem outside the loop by checking if base == 0 and nrservs != 0 before setting remain to serv->sv_nrpools.
reviews.raw_body:
svc_pool_map_init_pernode() uses for_each_node_with_cpus() so we can be
certain that each node which has been allocated a pool will have at
least 1 cpu.  Thus that last condition isn't needed.

I would probably address the problem outside the loop with

if (base == 0 && nrservs != 0)
   /* We need at least one thread per pool for correct functionality */
   remain = serv->sv_nrpools;

or similar.  But your version works too and this isn't
performance-critical code.

Reviewed-by: NeilBrown <neil@brown.name>

Thanks,
NeilBrown

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown suggested discarding the sv_nrpools field to eliminate a modulus operation in svc_pool_for_cpu(), which is no longer necessary.
reviews.raw_body:
I would rather discard ->sv_nrpools as described in previous reply, then
this modulus would disappear as it isn't needed.

Thanks,
NeilBrown

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch series hardcodes the pool mode to per-node, removing other modes. It addresses a shortcoming in the existing code by ensuring that RPCs are scheduled to pools with available threads and prevents routing requests to empty pools. Additionally, it optimizes the enqueueing codepath by eliminating modulus operations.

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch series hardcodes the pool mode to per-node, removing other modes. It addresses a shortcoming in the existing code by ensuring that RPCs are scheduled to pools with available threads and prevents routing requests to empty pools. Additionally, it optimizes the enqueueing codepath by eliminating modulus operations.
